title: AIAA Rocketry Summer Camp 2024
description: Rocketry Summer Camp under AIAA OC to provide foundational platform for rocketry enthusiasts and great opportunity to identify the future rocketeer.

title: American Rocketry Challenge 2025
description:
ARC (The American Rocketry Challenge) is the world’s largest student rocket contest, designed to inspire the next generation of engineers and aerospace experts. Open to middle and high school students in the United States, the competition tasks teams with designing, building, and launching model rockets that must meet specific, changing mission requirements each year—such as reaching a precise altitude or carrying a fragile "payload" (usually a raw egg) back to Earth without breaking.

We were a new team and this was  the first time for most people on the team. We aimed to work together, share ideas, get better each day, and strive for better data. The first few times, we had some trouble getting it all together at the beginning but as everyone started to learn more and gather more experience, the pace picked up and we could advance much faster. We would design a rocket, get it reviewed by our mentor, build it, launch it, gather data, and analyze the data thoroughly. Once this process was done, we would tweak the design a bit, adjust them in the actual rocket and re-launch it for another trial.

With determination and focus, we achieved an excellent qualification round which took us to National Finals. As the first timers, we take it to our pride to reach National Finals. At the end of it, we all came back with extraordinary learning about the end-to-end engineering process, various design tools, data analysis based engineering modification, team work & collaboration and most importantly solving various real-time problems keeping calm and patience.

title: American Rocketry Challenge 2026
description: We, The Saguaro Strikers (Scottsdale AZ ARC team for the 2025-26), are absolutely locked in right now. Our Arizona team is heading into our very first American Rocketry Challenge, and the mix of nerves and pure excitement is unreal. We know it's a tough challenge, but we are focused on making it to Nationals. We are a new team and this will be the first time for most people on the team. We aim to work together, share ideas, get better each day, and strive for better data. Honestly, that determination has become our secret weapon. We are just proving that a new team from the dry heat of Arizona can build an impressive machine. Now, we are ready to put all that spirit to the test and see our real, regulation-compliant rocket fly high for the first time. Our determination drives us for better results each time we fly and we aim for perfection. We feel that our attention to perfection and the smallest details sets us apart from many because it helps us to be the most precise and unlock 100% of the potential a new team can possibly have.

description: Keep watching this space for details about upcoming American Rocketry Challenge 2027

title: Remotely controlled exploration rover with robotic arm
description:
Challenges in performing hazardous or risk prone activities remotely on earth or under water resulting into loss of human lives. Also, inability to perform extraterrestrial exploration on celestial bodies not feasible to send human.
The solution is an exploring robot that has been designed for exploring purposes on land only for the time being. The prototype model is basically a rover that can be controlled remotely with a joystick module. The connection between the control center or joysticks and the rover is a Bluetooth with 98 feet range. The arm is controlled by a total of 2 joysticks and can go left-right, up-down, and forward-back (i.e. 3 degrees of motion). The chassis has a total of 6 wheels and 4 motors to help the rover move and the middle two wheels are for support of better weight distribution. Finally, the camera is the part that will send live feed to control center and will help to navigate through the unknown and maybe dangerous place.
